note: Labeled corpus for tuning the Signal Desk. Published posts are referenced live from src/content/blog/; rejected drafts are snapshot fixtures (their branches are gone). 'expected' is the verdict a human editor would defend under the current mandate. A verdict two notches off (PUBLISH vs REJECT) is a hard fail; one notch (PUBLISH vs REVISE) is a soft signal.

cases.note: Strong tech post with a real local angle (winter solar power budget). Watch: the 12h->47h / 8-hop figures are the accuracy soft spot.
Retrospective anchored on a non-event with no takeaway. The v1.5 'so what' rule (S4.4/S6) should flag it.

Genuinely useful and actionable, BUT v1.5 reclassifies it: the 'next one might not stay that small' fear-hook opener (§8) and the 'Why this belongs on a radio-and-mesh site' meta-section (§8) are now both banned. Strong topic, fixable execution. (Label was PUBLISH pre-v1.5.)
Comparison-trap: laundered coverage claim about our mesh via a competitor's weakness (S10.4).
Well-sourced but thin, generic local relevance; weak 'so what'. Fixable, not a reject.
Comparison-trap again, plus the false overhead-vs-terrestrial physics symmetry.
